grants.title: SBRI Healthcare: themed competitions
grants.summary: Themed competitions for innovations addressing unmet NHS need. Open to digital, AI and device innovations. Includes an Investment Readiness programme.
grants.means: The flagship NHS-aligned non-dilutive funder. As much a credibility marker as a cash injection. An SBRI award signals NHS validation to later investors.

grants.title: NIHR Invention for Innovation (i4i): Product Development Awards
grants.source: National Institute for Health and Care Research
grants.value: SMEs eligible for up to 100% funding
grants.summary: Translational funding for medical devices, IVDs and digital health technologies bridging proof-of-concept to real-world clinical adoption. Two-stage application.
grants.means: Best fit for a product with early clinical evidence that needs the push to adoption-readiness. The two-stage process means an outline can be tested cheaply before committing to a full bid.

grants.title: NIHR Efficacy and Mechanism Evaluation (EME) Programme
grants.source: National Institute for Health and Care Research
grants.summary: Funds evaluation of efficacy and the mechanisms behind health interventions, including digital and technology-enabled interventions.
grants.means: Relevant where the gap is hard clinical evidence rather than product build. Outline applications close 5 August 2026.

grants.title: Innovate UK: Biomedical Catalyst
grants.summary: Supports SMEs developing innovative solutions to health and healthcare challenges, from small projects through to large collaborative R&D.
grants.means: The broadest UK non-dilutive R&D funder for healthtech. Pairs well with SBRI/NIHR (different stages of the same journey).

grants.title: EIC Accelerator
grants.summary: Grant plus equity for deep-tech SMEs and startups. UK organisations are eligible following association to Horizon Europe.
grants.means: Six fixed cut-offs a year, predictable enough to plan a submission around. Next cut-off 8 July 2026; further cut-offs 2 September and 4 November.

grants.title: Horizon Europe: Health Cluster (Cluster 1)
grants.summary: Collaborative health research and innovation funding. UK organisations can apply as part of a consortium of at least three countries, one an EU member state.
grants.means: Big money, but consortium-bound. Best approached as a partner in a larger bid rather than a solo route. The 2026-27 work programme is published; specific calls have fixed deadlines.

grants.title: NIHR i4i Connect
grants.source: National Institute for Health and Care Research
grants.summary: Smaller, more accessible sibling to NIHR i4i Product Development Awards. Bridges earlier-stage device and digital health work towards adoption readiness.
grants.means: Best fit for SMEs with promising tech but pre-clinical-evidence. Cheaper to apply for than full Product Development Awards. Use it as a stepping stone to the full i4i scheme.

grants.title: NIHR Programme Grants for Applied Research
grants.source: National Institute for Health and Care Research
grants.summary: Large multi-year applied research grants for programmes addressing NHS priorities. Two-stage application: outline then full bid.
grants.means: Big money for university partnerships. Healthtech SMEs typically join as industry partners on academic-led bids rather than leading their own. Worth knowing which research groups are bidding in your therapeutic area.

grants.title: NIHR Health Technology Assessment (HTA) Programme
grants.source: National Institute for Health and Care Research
grants.summary: Funds research that produces the evidence base for the NHS, including clinical effectiveness, cost-effectiveness and broader impact of health technologies.
grants.means: The route to fund a definitive clinical trial of your digital health technology. Long timelines (years), academic-led, but produces NICE-grade evidence that unlocks reimbursement.

grants.title: Innovate UK Smart Grants
grants.summary: Open-themed grants for game-changing innovations from UK SMEs in any sector. Health and digital health applications are eligible and well-represented.
grants.means: The most flexible Innovate UK route. Open theme means you set the scope. Health-focused bids do well if they show clear NHS demand and commercial viability. Multiple rounds a year, predictable cycle.

grants.title: Wellcome Innovator Awards
grants.summary: Funds early-to-mid-stage translation of basic research into health innovations. Open to both academics and SMEs in partnership.
grants.means: Strong fit if your tech is grounded in scientific discovery, not just engineering. Wellcome values the underlying research story as much as the product. Good for therapeutic AI, novel diagnostics, biosignal work.

grants.title: MRC Confidence in Concept
grants.summary: Block-funded scheme distributed via universities and research institutes to support early-stage translation. Funds go to internal CiC competitions at each host.
grants.means: Indirect route. You apply via your university or research partner, not directly to MRC. Useful for de-risking a digital health concept before chasing larger NIHR or Innovate UK funding.

grants.title: LifeArc Translational Awards
grants.summary: Medical research charity that funds translation of academic research into patient impact. Active in diagnostics, rare disease, and digital therapeutics.
grants.means: Charity money with industry-style rigour. Best fit for diagnostic and therapeutic devices with a clear unmet-need narrative. Less common for general-purpose health software.

grants.title: Health Foundation: Q Exchange
grants.summary: Small grants for NHS quality improvement projects from members of the Q community. Funds collaborative ideas chosen by community vote.
grants.means: Small money but useful pilot funding if you have an NHS partner inside Q. Builds frontline relationships and produces case studies. Not a route to scale, but a route to credibility.

grants.title: British Heart Foundation Translational Awards
grants.summary: Cardiovascular disease-specific funding for translational research and innovation, including device and digital health work with a cardiac focus.
grants.means: Only relevant if your product has a clear cardiology angle. BHF money is competitive but the disease focus narrows the field dramatically. Pairs well with NHS cardiac network engagement.

grants.title: Cancer Research UK Innovation Awards
grants.summary: Portfolio of grants for cancer research and innovation, including early detection, diagnostics, treatment and digital tools.
grants.means: Cancer-specific. Strong fit for AI-in-imaging, early detection biomarkers, and digital decision support in oncology. Look for the Convergence Sciences and Early Detection schemes specifically.

grants.title: Health Data Research UK funding calls
grants.summary: UK-wide programme funding work on health data infrastructure, federated analytics, and trusted research environments. Periodic open calls.
grants.means: Niche but valuable if your work touches health data infrastructure, Trusted Research Environments, or federated analytics. Often co-funded with NIHR or industry partners.

grants.title: DHSC Policy Innovation Funds (various)
grants.source: Department of Health and Social Care
grants.summary: Periodic policy-driven funding pots from DHSC tied to current strategy (AI diagnostic funds, 10 Year Plan allocations, workforce productivity funds). Hard to track centrally.
grants.means: The most opaque category. Pots appear, often announced via ministerial speeches or NHS England circulars, with short deadlines. Following DHSC press releases and NHS England announcements is the only way to spot them in time. High editorial value here.

grants.title: SBRI Healthcare: AI in Health and Care themed call (2022)
grants.value: Phase 1 up to £100k; Phase 2 up to £1m
grants.summary: SBRI Healthcare's themed competition focused on AI in health and care. Funded Phase 1 feasibility and Phase 2 development awards.
grants.means: Closed call, but a useful reference point. SBRI typically rotates themes year on year; an AI-focused round is likely again. The 2022 alumni list shows where SBRI saw maturity in AI for NHS use cases.

grants.title: Innovate UK Biomedical Catalyst (2024 round)
grants.summary: The 2024 round of Innovate UK's Biomedical Catalyst, funding industry-led collaborative R&D in health innovation. Annual cadence.
grants.means: Closed for 2024, but the cycle is reliable. Use the 2024 awardee list to spot which subsectors Innovate UK is currently betting on, and plan a 2026 submission against that pattern.

grants.title: NHSX AI Award (Round 3)
grants.summary: Round 3 of the NHSX AI in Health and Care Award. Funded translation through to real-world clinical evaluation for AI products with credible evidence.
grants.means: Closed. Programme is now wound into the broader NIHR pathway. The Round 3 awardee list is the most relevant snapshot of what survived NHSX's rigour.
